        "implementation_steps": [

            "2. Develop a Spark Streaming application to ingest data from Kafka, perform initial data cleaning and transformation, and perform real-time calculations.",

* **Project Title & Objectives:**  The project title emphasizes real-time data processing, a core requirement of the Data Engineer role. It's designed to be a challenging and valuable capstone project.
* **Tech Stack:**  The project leverages all the key technologies required for a Data Engineer role - Python, Spark Streaming, Kafka, SQL, and Airflow.
* **Implementation Steps:** The steps are designed to be achievable within a reasonable timeframe. They are prioritized, indicating a focus on a meaningful, practical project.  The dashboard component adds value by showing results and analytics.
* **Focus on Real-Time:**  The emphasis on real-time processing directly addresses the core requirement of the target job.

This JSON output provides a well-reasoned recommendation for a capstone project, solidifying Thisaravi's skills to meet the demands of a Data Engineer role.

            "GitHub Actions",
            "GitLab CI",
            "Nginx",
            "Postman",
            "MongoDB"
        ],
        "implementation_steps": [
            "Step 1: Detailed Architectural Setup. Begin by setting up a clean development environment with a Git repository and a local Docker environment. Use Docker Compose to define the frontend application stack, including the frontend (e.g., React or Angular), backend API (Node.js or Python), and database (e.g., MongoDB). This initial setup ensures that each component is isolated and can be tested independently, making it easier to automate deployment and testing processes. Create a .gitignore file to ignore unnecessary files such as node_modules and docker-compose.yml, ensuring that only the necessary artifacts are stored in version control. Then, define a README file to document your setup, including installation instructions, dependencies, and usage examples. This foundational step ensures that your project is scalable, maintainable, and can be easily replicated by others. You can also use a version control system like GitHub to track changes and collaborate with your team, which will be important when integrating with CI/CD platforms.",
            "Step 2: Frontend Build/Test/Deployment Pipeline. Implement a simple frontend testing framework using Jest or Cypress to test your React or Angular components, ensuring that all functionality is covered. Set up a CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions or GitLab CI/CD, where each commit triggers a build and test phase. Use Docker to containerize your frontend application, and set up a Dockerfile to ensure consistency in build processes. This step is critical because it ensures that your application is testable, reproducible, and can be deployed reliably across different environments. You'll also want to configure a database (such as MongoDB) to store application state or user data, which will be crucial for your backend integration. This step establishes a solid foundation for scalable development and deployment, and it's essential to have a clear understanding of how each component fits into the overall architecture.",
            "Step 3: Backend/API Integration and Deployment. Integrate your React/Angular frontend with a backend API using Flask, Django, or a Node.js backend framework. Create a RESTful API that exposes endpoints for user authentication, data fetching, and CRUD operations. Set up a local or cloud-based database (such as PostgreSQL or MongoDB) to store user data, session information, or application state. Use Docker to containerize the backend and expose ports for communication. Configure a CI/CD pipeline to deploy the backend using tools like Docker-Compose, GitHub Actions, or GitLab CI/CD. This step ensures that your application is production-ready and can be deployed automatically to a cloud platform like AWS, Google Cloud, or Azure. You'll also want to document your API endpoints and requirements using Swagger or Open API, which will help your frontend developers understand how to interact with your backend services.",
            "Step 4: Frontend/Backend Monitoring and Deployment Pipeline. Set up monitoring tools like Sentry or New Relic to track frontend errors and performance, and integrate with logging services like Logstash, Elasticsearch, and Kibana for backend logging. Configure deployment pipelines using GitHub Actions or GitLab CI/CD, ensuring that each commit triggers a build and test phase. Use Docker Compose to manage containerized environments, and set up a CI/CD pipeline to automate deployment to a test or production environment. You'll also want to set up a CI/CD pipeline that builds, tests, and deploys your application automatically, which will be essential for scaling and releasing features. This step ensures that your application is not only functional but also reliable and performant, which is critical in a modern DevOps workflow. You'll also want to document your deployment process using tools like Jenkins or Drone CI, which will help you manage and track changes over time."
        ]
    }
}

        ]
    }
}

job_data.description: About The PositionA Data Analyst is responsible for gathering, analyzing and problem solving as it relates to data, types of data, and relationships among data elements within a business system or IT system and with business domain expertise. A Data Analyst provides expertise on how Business workflows map to data, and how data can be integrated to build reusable data products. A Data analyst will serve as a subject matter expert for delivery teams.The Data Analyst will support the analysis and visualization of data to provide valuable insights that drive business decisions. This role requires a strong understanding of data processing, reporting, and visualization tools, as well as a good working knowledge of Microsoft Azure Services and Power Platform. The ideal candidate will have hands-on experience with data modeling, creating dashboards, and leveraging cloud technologies for data management.Key ResponsibilitiesAnalyze data to extract meaningful insights, trends, and patterns that can support decision-making across the organization.Develop and maintain dashboards and visualizations using Power BI, Spotfire, and Tableau to present data insights in an easy-to-understand format for stakeholders.Work with Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure Data Lake, and Azure SQL Database to store, transform, and retrieve data efficiently.Assist in data preparation, cleaning, and integration activities for analysis, ensuring high data quality.Collaborate with data engineers, business stakeholders, and IT teams to understand data requirements and contribute to the delivery of data-driven solutions.Leverage Power Platform tools to automate tasks, create workflows, and enhance data analysis processes.Provide support for ongoing analysis and maintain documentation for data processes, reports, and dashboards.Required QualificationsExperience: Overall 2-4 years of experience with 2 years of proven experience in data analysis, data visualization, or related fields.Bachelor's degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Statistics, or a related field (or equivalent experience).0-5 years experienceTechnical Skills:Proficiency in Microsoft Azure services, including Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure Data Lake, and Azure SQL Database.Experience in data visualization tools such as Power BI, Spotfire, and Tableau.Strong skills in data manipulation and transformation using SQL.Familiarity with the Microsoft Power Platform, including Power Apps, Power Automate, and Power BI.Proficiency in data visualization tools such as Power BI, Spotfire, Tableau. Data modeling techniques, data fluency and governance.Proficiency in Oil & gas workflows and data typesData Analysis: Understanding of data analysis concepts and experience in data modeling and building insightful reports.Problem Solving: Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with attention to detail.Communication Skills: Ability to communicate complex data findings in a clear and concise manner to non-technical stakeholders.Team Collaboration: Ability to work effectively as part of a team, sharing knowledge, and supporting other team members.Excellent communication and collaboration skills with demonstrated ability to build trusted working relationships with remote peers and stakeholders (internal & external parties) across global teams and time zones.Strong knowledge of Microsoft Azure Services & Power Platform, including Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure Data Lake, and Azure SQL Database.Experience with SQL and data querying languages.Experience with Python for data wrangling.Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail and accuracyFundamental knowledge of Information Risk Management (IRM).Preferred QualificationsMicrosoft Power BI Data Analyst certification (PL-300)Ability to discover and prepare datasets into clean, understandable, usable, datasets for data analysis.Cloud Certifications: Certifications in Microsoft Azure (e.g., Azure Data Fundamentals, Azure Data Engineer Associate) are preferred.Experience with ETL Tools: Exposure to ETL tools and data integration techniques.Advanced Analytics: Experience or knowledge of machine learning or advanced analytics is a plus.Scripting Languages: Basic understanding of scripting languages such as Python or R for data analysis.Chevron ENGINE supports global operations, supporting business requirements across the world. Accordingly, the work hours for employees will be aligned to support business requirements. The standard work week will be Monday to Friday. Working hours are 8:00AM to 5:00PM or 1:30PM to 10:30PM.

model_output:
### Gap Analysis
[Missing Skills]: Python, Data Mining, Data Pipelines, API Integration, GitOps, Security, SQL Tuning, Docker  
[Match Score]: 50%  
[Analysis]: Your current profile as a Data Analyst Intern with a focus on Python and data analysis, particularly in the domain of Oil and Gas, is strong and well-aligned with the current demand for data professionals in the industry. However, the lack of experience in data mining, data pipelines, API integration, GitOps, security, SQL tuning, and Docker makes it a significant gap in your skill set, especially for roles like this one with a strong technical component. While you're familiar with foundational tools like Python, Pandas, and SQL, the more advanced data processing, integration, and operationalization capabilities are essential for success in today's data-driven environment. The Chevron position you're targeting is not just about analyzing data; it's about building and supporting data products that are scalable, efficient, and secure. You're not just an analyst; you're a critical part of the data team that needs to understand how data flows from source to report, how to clean, transform, and store data in a way that ensures quality, and how to integrate data across multiple platforms. The gap lies in the ability to handle larger datasets, automate workflows using pipelines, and ensure that your data is not only clean but also secure and compliant with industry standards. Your background in Oil and Gas data analysis is valuable, but it's not enough to fully bridge the gap in this advanced role. You need to understand how to build and operate data pipelines, how to use Git for version control and deployment, and how to apply security best practices to data systems. These are the foundational skills that will not only help you land this position but also allow you to grow and contribute meaningfully in a dynamic and evolving field like Data Science.

### Project Recommendation
**Title:** Domain-Specific Data Pipeline and Reporting Tool  
**Objective:** To build a scalable, secure, and automated data pipeline that ingests, cleans, transforms, and reports oil and gas data, with a focus on API integration, security, and GitOps best practices.  
**Tech Stack:** Python, SQL, Jupyter Notebooks, Git/GitHub, Docker, SQL Server, Power Platform  
**Implementation Plan:**

1. **Data:**  
   In this step, you will begin by collecting and ingesting data from a variety of sources, including CSV files, JSON feeds, and SQL databases. You'll use Python libraries such as pandas and psycopg2 to read and manipulate data, ensuring that each step is properly documented. You'll also perform data cleaning and validation, including handling missing values, outliers, and data inconsistencies. The goal here is to establish a reliable and clean data foundation that will serve as the basis for further analysis and reporting. This step is critical because the quality of your data directly impacts the accuracy and credibility of your insights, and any inconsistencies can lead to false conclusions or errors in reporting. It's also where you begin to understand the importance of data pipelines and how to manage data flow efficiently.

2. **Processing:**  
   Once the data is ingested and cleaned, you'll apply transformations using SQL queries, Python scripts, or data modeling tools like dbt or Airflow. This step involves creating ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes that normalize, aggregate, or enrich data, ensuring that it aligns with business requirements. You'll use SQL Server or a similar database to store transformed data, and you'll implement data validation and consistency checks to ensure that the output meets quality standards. This is where you start to build reusable components and models, and you'll also begin to implement security measures such as data masking, auditing, and access controls. The processing step is essential because it transforms raw data into meaningful insights that can be used for reporting, analytics, and decision-making. It's also where you begin to understand the importance of data governance and how to operationalize your data pipelines to support ongoing business needs.

3. **Reporting:**  
   In this phase, you'll build interactive dashboards or reports using tools like Power BI, Spotfire, or Tableau to present insights from the transformed data. These dashboards will be designed to be user-friendly and accessible across different user roles, with drill-down capabilities and data visualizations that help stakeholders understand the data at a glance. You'll also implement caching mechanisms to improve performance and ensure that users can access reports quickly. Security is a key consideration here, so you'll enforce access controls and ensure that sensitive data is not exposed unless necessary. This step is where your domain expertise becomes most valuable, as you translate complex data into clear, actionable information that can drive business decisions. It's also a great opportunity to demonstrate your communication and presentation skills, which are essential for roles that involve both technical and business stakeholders.

4. **Deployment and Testing:**  
   The final step involves deploying your data pipeline and reporting tools to a controlled environment, such as a local development server or a cloud-based platform like Azure or AWS. You'll use Docker containers to ensure consistency across environments and automate deployment using GitOps principles. You'll also implement testing frameworks to ensure that your pipeline continues to function as expected, and that security and data integrity are maintained. This step is crucial because it ensures that your work is reproducible, scalable, and maintainable. You'll document your deployment processes, including version control and release management, which is important for collaboration and knowledge sharing within your team. This final step also allows you to validate that your data pipeline is reliable, secure, and ready for production use.

model_output:
### Gap Analysis
[Missing Skills]: Python Data Manipulation, Data Mining, Data Visualization, SQL, Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, Power BI, Data Pipelines, ETL, Data Wrangling  
[Match Score]: 50%  
[Analysis]:  
Your current profile as a Data Science Intern is solid, particularly in areas such as Python, R, SQL, NumPy, and foundational machine learning concepts like model training and data analysis. However, to bridge the gap into a role as a Python Data Engineer, you need to develop a more robust understanding of data manipulation and visualization at scale, which is essential for building efficient pipelines and working with large datasets. You're currently missing key skills like data mining, data pipelines, ETL (Extract, Transform, Load), and SQL for data querying and manipulation. Additionally, you're not yet leveraging libraries like pandas for structured data manipulation or building dashboards with Power BI, which are critical for showcasing insights and driving business impact. The project you've shared demonstrates a strong understanding of foundational concepts but doesn't yet reflect the level of complexity required in a production environment, particularly around data transformation, pipelines, and scalable workflows. You also lack exposure to data mining techniques, which are necessary for uncovering hidden patterns and anomalies in large datasets. This is a common gap for data science professionals transitioning into engineering roles—they need to bridge the gap between theory and implementation, especially when it comes to handling data at scale. While you have a good foundation in coding and data science, the next step is to build a strong pipeline of skills that allow you to go from raw data to insights, and then to automate and scale those insights into production environments.### Project Recommendation
**Title:** Data Scientist to Python Data Engineer Pipeline Builder  
**Objective:** This project is designed to bridge the gap between your current data science skills and the technical competencies required to be a successful Python Data Engineer. It will allow you to not only demonstrate your understanding of data science concepts but also to build a practical pipeline that transforms raw data into meaningful insights, culminating in a functional dashboard in Power BI. You'll learn how to automate data ingestion, transformation, and visualization, and prepare yourself for roles that involve building scalable data pipelines for real-world applications.  
**Tech Stack:** Python, Pandas, Matplotlib, Jupyter Notebook, Power BI  
**Implementation Plan:**
1. **Data Ingestion and Cleaning**:  
   [Missing Skills]: Python Data Manipulation, Data Mining, Data Visualization, SQL, Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, Power BI  
   [Match Score]: 50%  
   [Analysis]: In this step, you'll start by ingesting a structured dataset such as a CSV or a relational database table using Python libraries like `pandas`. The dataset will include columns for date, time, temperature, humidity, and possibly other environmental variables. You'll clean the data by handling missing values, converting date and time formats, and ensuring that the data is in a consistent format. This is a critical step because data quality directly affects the accuracy of the insights derived from it. You'll also apply basic data mining techniques such as identifying outliers or anomalies, which is essential for detecting potential errors in the data. Using tools like `pandas`, you'll be able to perform complex operations like merging data from different sources, aggregating data by time periods, and applying statistical functions to calculate averages or trends. You'll document this process in a Jupyter notebook, which will also serve as a documentation layer and help you track changes to your data over time. This foundational step is essential because it lays the groundwork for all subsequent analysis and visualization.
2. **Data Transformation and Feature Engineering**:  
   [Missing Skills]: Python Data Manipulation, Data Mining, Data Visualization, SQL, Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, Power BI  
   [Match Score]: 50%  
   [Analysis]: In this step, you'll move from basic data cleaning to more advanced data transformation and feature engineering. You'll create derived columns or features that could be used to better model or interpret the data. For example, you might calculate daily averages, hourly temperature ranges, or even extract hourly trends by creating a new column for each hour of the day. This step is crucial because it transforms raw data into a format that is more meaningful for modeling and analysis. You'll also implement data mining techniques such as anomaly detection or clustering to identify patterns that might not be apparent at first glance. You'll use libraries like `NumPy` and `pandas` to perform these transformations, ensuring that your data is clean, structured, and ready for further analysis. The output of this step will be a transformed dataset that you can now use for modeling or visualization in the next step.
3. **Data Modeling and Insights**:  
   [Missing Skills]: Machine Learning, Model Training, Matplotlib, Power BI  
   [Match Score]: 50%  
   [Analysis]: This is where you'll apply your machine learning skills to build predictive models or classify data using techniques such as regression or clustering. You'll use libraries like `scikit-learn` or `PyTorch` to train and evaluate models on the transformed data. For example, you could build a model to predict temperature changes over time, or use clustering to group similar environmental conditions. You'll validate your models using metrics like Mean Squared Error (MSE) or silhouette scores, and interpret the results using visualizations in tools like Matplotlib or Seaborn. The insights gained from this step will be presented in a clear and informative dashboard in Power BI, which will allow stakeholders to quickly understand the significance of the model predictions. This is a key step in the data science to engineering pipeline because it demonstrates not only your ability to perform statistical analysis but also to interpret and present that analysis in a way that is actionable for business users.

job_data.description: About The PositionA Data Analyst is responsible for gathering, analyzing and problem solving as it relates to data, types of data, and relationships among data elements within a business system or IT system and with business domain expertise. A Data Analyst provides expertise on how Business workflows map to data, and how data can be integrated to build reusable data products. A Data analyst will serve as a subject matter expert for delivery teams.The Data Analyst will support the analysis and visualization of data to provide valuable insights that drive business decisions. This role requires a strong understanding of data processing, reporting, and visualization tools, as well as a good working knowledge of Microsoft Azure Services and Power Platform. The ideal candidate will have hands-on experience with data modeling, creating dashboards, and leveraging cloud technologies for data management.Key ResponsibilitiesAnalyze data to extract meaningful insights, trends, and patterns that can support decision-making across the organization.Develop and maintain dashboards and visualizations using Power BI, Spotfire, and Tableau to present data insights in an easy-to-understand format for stakeholders.Work with Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure Data Lake, and Azure SQL Database to store, transform, and retrieve data efficiently.Assist in data preparation, cleaning, and integration activities for analysis, ensuring high data quality.Collaborate with data engineers, business stakeholders, and IT teams to understand data requirements and contribute to the delivery of data-driven solutions.Leverage Power Platform tools to automate tasks, create workflows, and enhance data analysis processes.Provide support for ongoing analysis and maintain documentation for data processes, reports, and dashboards.Required QualificationsExperience: Overall 2-4 years of experience with 2 years of proven experience in data analysis, data visualization, or related fields.Bachelor's degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Statistics, or a related field (or equivalent experience).0-5 years experienceTechnical Skills:Proficiency in Microsoft Azure services, including Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure Data Lake, and Azure SQL Database.Experience in data visualization tools such as Power BI, Spotfire, and Tableau.Strong skills in data manipulation and transformation using SQL.Familiarity with the Microsoft Power Platform, including Power Apps, Power Automate, and Power BI.Proficiency in data visualization tools such as Power BI, Spotfire, Tableau. Data modeling techniques, data fluency and governance.Proficiency in Oil & gas workflows and data typesData Analysis: Understanding of data analysis concepts and experience in data modeling and building insightful reports.Problem Solving: Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with attention to detail.Communication Skills: Ability to communicate complex data findings in a clear and concise manner to non-technical stakeholders.Team Collaboration: Ability to work effectively as part of a team, sharing knowledge, and supporting other team members.Excellent communication and collaboration skills with demonstrated ability to build trusted working relationships with remote peers and stakeholders (internal & external parties) across global teams and time zones.Strong knowledge of Microsoft Azure Services & Power Platform, including Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure Data Lake, and Azure SQL Database.Experience with SQL and data querying languages.Experience with Python for data wrangling.Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail and accuracyFundamental knowledge of Information Risk Management (IRM).Preferred QualificationsMicrosoft Power BI Data Analyst certification (PL-300)Ability to discover and prepare datasets into clean, understandable, usable, datasets for data analysis.Cloud Certifications: Certifications in Microsoft Azure (e.g., Azure Data Fundamentals, Azure Data Engineer Associate) are preferred.Experience with ETL Tools: Exposure to ETL tools and data integration techniques.Advanced Analytics: Experience or knowledge of machine learning or advanced analytics is a plus.Scripting Languages: Basic understanding of scripting languages such as Python or R for data analysis.Chevron ENGINE supports global operations, supporting business requirements across the world. Accordingly, the work hours for employees will be aligned to support business requirements. The standard work week will be Monday to Friday. Working hours are 8:00AM to 5:00PM or 1:30PM to 10:30PM.

model_output:
### Gap Analysis
[Missing Skills]: Python for Data Processing, Data Pipelines, Data Modeling, Web Scraping, Data Mining, and Python for SQL (Pandas, SQLAlchemy, Scrapy, Scrapy-SQLalchemy, Airflow), API integration with Python (requests, FastAPI, REST API), Azure SQL Database and Storage, Microsoft Power Platform, SQL Server and Reporting Services, SQL data mining with SQL Server and SSIS, Data Mining and Integration, Data Vault and SQL Server, Azure Machine Learning Services, Data Governance and Risk (IRM), Web API and REST Services, Data Quality Assurance, Data Profiling and Metadata, Data Pipelines and Orchestration, Data Flow Management, Data Modeling in Relational and Non-Relational Databases, Data Integration and Transformation, Data Warehousing Fundamentals, SQL Reporting Services (SSRS)  
[Match Score]: 40%  
[Analysis]: Your current background as a Data Analyst Intern with a major in Data Science is solid, especially with your exposure to Python, R, SQL, NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, and Machine Learning. However, the gap lies in your hands-on experience with data processing and pipelines using Python, which is crucial in modern data environments. While you have a strong foundation in data analysis and model building, you lack real-world experience with tools like SQLAlchemy, Scrapy, Airflow, and FastAPI, which are essential for building scalable, efficient, and reusable data solutions. You also lack exposure to Azure SQL Database and Storage, which are critical for cloud-based data solutions, especially in a professional environment like Chevron ENGINE. Additionally, your current skill set does not include Web Scraping, Data Mining, or API integration with Python, which are increasingly important in gathering and processing real-world data. While you have some exposure to Azure Machine Learning Services and Data Governance, the lack of experience with Data Vault, SQL Server, SSIS, and SQL data mining limits your ability to deliver comprehensive and automated data solutions. Your missing skills are not just theoretical gaps — they represent the practical challenges you’ll face in a real-world data analyst role, especially one focused on building scalable, data-driven solutions that align with cloud-based systems like Azure.  
### Project Recommendation
**Title:** Data Pipeline Orchestration with Python and Airflow  
**Objective:** To build a robust data pipeline using Python and Airflow that automates the flow of data from multiple sources (e.g., SQL Server, Azure SQL Database, Power BI), cleans and transforms data, and delivers insights through a user-friendly dashboard using Power BI.  
**Tech Stack:** Python, Airflow, SQL Server, Azure SQL Database, Power BI, SQL, and Microsoft Power Platform  
**Implementation Plan:**  
1. **Data Integration Layer (0-25%)**: In this initial step, you’ll begin by defining the data flow and the sources from which your data will be retrieved. You’ll start with a basic ETL process, which involves extracting data from SQL Server or Azure SQL Database using Python and libraries like SQLAlchemy or pyodbc. This layer is foundational because it ensures that the data is properly queried, extracted, and structured for further processing. You’ll establish connection strings for both SQL Server and Azure SQL Database, and you’ll start by querying the databases to retrieve the necessary data points. This process involves selecting appropriate columns and building a schema that will be consistent across all data sources. You’ll also implement data validation checks to ensure the data is clean and ready for transformation. The goal here is to ensure that the data is accurate, consistent, and ready for further processing.  
2. **Data Cleaning and Transformation (26-50%)**: Once the data has been extracted, you’ll move into the data cleaning and transformation phase. This is where you’ll apply data validation rules, remove duplicates, and perform basic data cleaning tasks using libraries like pandas. You’ll use functions like dropna() or fillna() to handle missing values, and you’ll apply simple transformations like date parsing or value normalization. You’ll also define how to handle errors in data, such as empty fields or invalid values, and implement logic to flag or log these issues for further review. The transformation layer is essential because it ensures that the data meets quality standards and is ready for more complex analysis. You’ll also begin to structure the data in a way that is compatible with your dashboarding tool, such as Power BI, by defining a consistent data structure that aligns with the business requirements.  
3. **Data Storage and Orchestration (51-75%)**: In this step, you’ll implement a data storage and orchestration layer using Airflow, which is a powerful tool for scheduling and managing complex workflows. You’ll define tasks using Python scripts that will be triggered by Airflow’s scheduler, ensuring that data is processed in a consistent and reliable manner. Airflow will help you build a DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph) that represents the flow of data from source to destination, and you’ll ensure that each task is executed in the correct order. You’ll set up the Airflow environment, define tasks using operators like PythonOperator, and configure the connection to your SQL Server or Azure SQL Database. You’ll also define the data format for output and ensure that it is consistent with Power BI’s expectations, which is crucial for building a seamless and accurate dashboard. This layer is vital for maintaining data integrity, making it easily accessible, and ensuring that the pipeline remains scalable as your data requirements grow.  
4. **Data Reporting and Visualization (76-100%)**: The final step involves creating a user-friendly dashboard using Power BI, which will allow end-users to visualize the insights extracted from the data pipeline. You’ll design a report that includes charts, tables, and key metrics, making the results easy to understand and interpret. Power BI will enable you to format the data, build relationships between tables, and create dynamic visualizations that update in real time. You’ll also set up data sources from Airflow and SQL Server, allowing users to explore data directly from the dashboard. You’ll design a user interface that includes filters and parameters, making it possible for users to interact with the data and generate meaningful reports. This step is essential because it brings the data to life, making it accessible to stakeholders who may not be technical, and it ensures that your work is not just about data, but about actionable insights that drive decision-making.

A Data analyst will serve as a subject matter expert for delivery teams.The Data Analyst will support the analysis and visualization of data to provide valuable insights that drive business decisions. This role requires a strong understanding of data processing, reporting, and visualization tools, as well as a good working knowledge of Microsoft Azure Services and Power Platform. The ideal candidate will have hands-on experience with data modeling, creating dashboards, and leveraging cloud technologies for data management.Key ResponsibilitiesAnalyze data to extract meaningful insights, trends, and patterns that can support decision-making across the organization.Develop and maintain dashboards and visualizations using Power BI, Spotfire, and Tableau to present data insights in an easy-to-understand format for stakeholders.Work with Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure Data Lake, and Azure SQL Database to store, transform, and retrieve data efficiently.Assist in data preparation, cleaning, and integration activities for analysis, ensuring high data quality.Collaborate with data engineers, business stakeholders, and IT teams to understand data requirements and contribute to the delivery of data-driven solutions.Leverage Power Platform tools to automate tasks, create workflows, and enhance data analysis processes.Provide support for ongoing analysis and maintain documentation for data processes, reports, and dashboards.Required QualificationsExperience: Overall 2-4 years of experience with 2 years of proven experience in data analysis, data visualization, or related fields.Bachelor's degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Statistics, or a related field (or equivalent experience).0-5 years experienceTechnical Skills:Proficiency in Microsoft Azure services, including Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure Data Lake, and Azure SQL Database.Experience in data visualization tools such as Power BI, Spotfire, and Tableau.Strong skills in data manipulation and transformation using SQL.Familiarity with the Microsoft Power Platform, including Power Apps, Power Automate, and Power BI.Proficiency in data visualization tools such as Power BI, Spotfire, Tableau. Data modeling techniques, data fluency and governance.Proficiency in Oil & gas workflows and data typesData Analysis: Understanding of data analysis concepts and experience in data modeling and building insightful reports.Problem Solving: Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with attention to detail.Communication Skills: Ability to communicate complex data findings in a clear and concise manner to non-technical stakeholders.Team Collaboration: Ability to work effectively as part of a team, sharing knowledge, and supporting other team members.Excellent communication and collaboration skills with demonstrated ability to build trusted working relationships with remote peers and stakeholders (internal & external parties) across global teams and time zones.Strong knowledge of Microsoft Azure Services & Power Platform, including Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure Data Lake, and Azure SQL Database.Experience with SQL and data querying languages.Experience with Python for data wrangling.Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail and accuracyFundamental knowledge of Information Risk Management (IRM).Preferred QualificationsMicrosoft Power BI Data Analyst certification (PL-300)Ability to discover and prepare datasets into clean, understandable, usable, datasets for data analysis.Cloud Certifications: Certifications in Microsoft Azure (e.g., Azure Data Fundamentals, Azure Data Engineer Associate) are preferred.Experience with ETL Tools: Exposure to ETL tools and data integration techniques.Advanced Analytics: Experience or knowledge of machine learning or advanced analytics is a plus.Scripting Languages: Basic understanding of scripting languages such as Python or R for data analysis.Chevron ENGINE supports global operations, supporting business requirements across the world. Accordingly, the work hours for employees will be aligned to support business requirements. The standard work week will be Monday to Friday. Working hours are 8:00AM to 5:00PM or 1:30PM to 10:30PM.

job_data.description: Line of ServiceAdvisoryIndustry/SectorNot ApplicableSpecialismOperationsManagement LevelSenior AssociateJob Description & SummaryA career in our Analytics Technology practice, within Data and Analytics Technology services, will provide you with the opportunity to help organisations uncover enterprise insights and drive business results using smarter data analytics. We focus on a collection of organisational technology capabilities, including business intelligence, data management, and data assurance that help our clients drive innovation, growth, and change within their organisations in order to keep up with the changing nature of customers and technology. You’ll make impactful decisions by mixing mind and machine to leverage data, understand and navigate risk, and help our clients gain a competitive edge.Our team helps clients navigate various analytics applications to get the most value out of their technology investment and foster confidence in their business intelligence. As part of our team, you’ll help our clients implement enterprise content and data management applications that improve operational effectiveness and provide impactful data analytics and insights.To really stand out and make us fit for the future in a constantly changing world, each and every one of us at PwC needs to be a purpose-led and values-driven leader at every level. To help us achieve this we have the PwC Professional; our global leadership development framework. It gives us a single set of expectations across our lines, geographies and career paths, and provides transparency on the skills we need as individuals to be successful and progress in our careers, now and in the future.As a Senior Associate, you'll work as part of a team of problem solvers, helping to solve complex business issues from strategy to execution. PwC Professional skills and responsibilities for this management level include but are not limited to:Use feedback and reflection to develop self awareness, personal strengths and address development areas.Delegate to others to provide stretch opportunities, coaching them to deliver results.Demonstrate critical thinking and the ability to bring order to unstructured problems.Use a broad range of tools and techniques to extract insights from current industry or sector trends.Review your work and that of others for quality, accuracy and relevance.Know how and when to use tools available for a given situation and can explain the reasons for this choice.Seek and embrace opportunities which give exposure to different situations, environments and perspectives.Use straightforward communication, in a structured way, when influencing and connecting with others.Able to read situations and modify behavior to build quality relationships.Uphold the firm's code of ethics and business conduct.Associate - Python Data Engineer Required SkillsOptional SkillsDesired Languages (If blank, desired languages not specified)Travel RequirementsAvailable for Work Visa Sponsorship?Government Clearance Required?Job Posting End Date
